content:
{
  "title": "Meta-Prompting with Real-Time Counterfactual Feedback for Bias Mitigation in Large Language Models",
  "problem": "Current prompting techniques for reducing social bias in LLMs either require post-hoc interventions or cannot provide real-time guidance as outputs are generated. There is a need for prompting schemes that enable LLMs to introspect and adaptively mitigate social biases during generation, without reliance on model retraining or external datasets.",
  "motivation": "As LLMs are deployed in increasingly sensitive applications, social bias and harmful stereotypes remain persistent risks, undermining trust and fairness. Existing debiasing methods rely on static prompts or external knowledge and often lack dynamism in generation. A meta-prompting approach that dynamically injects counterfactual feedback during text generation promises more robust bias mitigation, tailored to the context and output.",
  "method": "We introduce a prompting framework that wraps user prompts with a meta-prompt that instructs the LLM to generate its primary response in parallel with a real-time counterfactual response (e.g., 'How would this response look if the subject’s gender, ethnicity, or background were different?'). The model then self-compares both outputs and immediately revises its primary response if disparities suggesting bias are detected. This process is fully implemented in the prompt layer—no model weights or training data are altered—and all reasoning is performed in-context, leveraging the LLM’s own capabilities, with iterative self-revision cycles until a bias threshold is met.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate this method on existing stereotype and bias detection datasets (e.g., StereoSet, CrowS-Pairs). Baselines include standard prompting, self-debiasing, and causal intervention-based prompting. Metrics will include automatic bias scores, human evaluation of stereotype presence/absence, and task relevance. Additional ablation studies will measure the effect of different counterfactual strategies and self-revision cycles."
}
